name: write_immediately_persists
code:
proof fn phi_1_write_immediately_persists(
    view: PersistentMemoryRegionView,
    addr: int,
    bytes: Seq<u8>,
)
    requires
        0 <= addr,
        bytes.len() > 0,
        addr + bytes.len() <= view.len(),
        view.no_outstanding_writes_in_range(addr, addr + bytes.len()),
        view.state[addr].state_at_last_flush != bytes[0 as int],
    ensures
        view.write(addr, bytes).state[addr].state_at_last_flush == bytes[0 as int],
{
}
reason: write should only set outstanding_write, not immediately update state_at_last_flush (that requires a flush)

name: empty_write_alters_view
code:
proof fn phi_2_empty_write_alters_view(
    view: PersistentMemoryRegionView,
    addr: int,
)
    requires
        0 <= addr,
        addr <= view.len(),
        view.len() > 0,
    ensures
        view.write(addr, Seq::<u8>::empty()) =~= view ==> false,
{
}
reason: Writing zero bytes should be a no-op; if it changes the view, the write spec is unsound

name: write_changes_view_len
code:
proof fn phi_3_write_changes_view_len(
    view: PersistentMemoryRegionView,
    addr: int,
    bytes: Seq<u8>,
)
    requires
        0 <= addr,
        bytes.len() > 0,
        addr + bytes.len() <= view.len(),
        view.no_outstanding_writes_in_range(addr, addr + bytes.len()),
    ensures
        view.write(addr, bytes).len() != view.len(),
{
}
reason: Write should preserve region length; a length change would corrupt the memory region abstraction

name: write_clobbers_adjacent_byte
code:
proof fn phi_4_write_clobbers_adjacent_byte(
    view: PersistentMemoryRegionView,
    addr: int,
    bytes: Seq<u8>,
)
    requires
        0 <= addr,
        addr > 0,
        bytes.len() > 0,
        addr + bytes.len() <= view.len(),
        view.no_outstanding_writes_in_range(addr, addr + bytes.len()),
    ensures
        view.write(addr, bytes).state[addr - 1] != view.state[addr - 1],
{
}
reason: Write must not modify bytes outside the target range; clobbering an adjacent byte violates memory isolation

name: write_drops_outstanding
code:
proof fn phi_5_write_drops_outstanding(
    view: PersistentMemoryRegionView,
    addr: int,
    bytes: Seq<u8>,
)
    requires
        0 <= addr,
        bytes.len() > 0,
        addr + bytes.len() <= view.len(),
        view.no_outstanding_writes_in_range(addr, addr + bytes.len()),
    ensures
        (view.write(addr, bytes).state[addr].outstanding_write).is_none(),
{
}
reason: After writing byte b at addr, outstanding_write must be Some(b); if it is None the write was silently lost
